topic: Portugal 1-1 DR Congo — Yoane Wissa scores DR Congo's first-ever World Cup goal, holding Portugal to a draw in Group K

hook.spoken_hook: Fifty-two years since their last World Cup. They had never scored a single goal.

scenes.voiceover: Fifty-two years since their last World Cup. Not one goal in their history. Then Houston happened.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, deep black background, gold gradient accents (#F7D774 to #C8881B). Moodly floodlight bokeh behind a bold white number '52' in condensed type. A single white line below: 'YEARS. ZERO GOALS.' Premium broadcast tease, no faces, no logos.

scenes.voiceover: Portugal, one of the tournament favorites, scored inside five minutes. Joao Neves, twenty-one years old. Ronaldo's sixth World Cup was off and running.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, deep black with gold accents, a bright white energy line slicing diagonally across the frame, bold condensed caption bar reading 'POR 1-0 COD'. The energy of a favorite striking early. Tense, premium broadcast feel, no faces.

scenes.voiceover: Then a Newcastle forward changed everything. Yoane Wissa — DR Congo's first ever World Cup goal. Fifty-two years of waiting, over in one swing.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, explosive gold burst on deep black, bold white condensed type reading '1-1', radiant gold light rays emanating from the center, confetti specks. A historic moment rendered in pure brand style with no faces or broadcast imagery.

scenes.voiceover: Portugal threw everyone at them. Ronaldo, Bruno, Leao. DR Congo would not break.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, deep black with tense gold-edged atmosphere, bold condensed white caption bar reading 'POR 1-1 COD'. A staredown mood — two shields facing off. Premium broadcast feel, no faces, no logos.

scenes.voiceover: From Zaire, 1974, to holding Portugal. This was a statement.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, deep black fading into gold at the bottom, bold white condensed 'HISTORY' in the center with radiating gold lines. Congratulatory, monumental, premium. Olympian mood, no broadcast imagery.

scenes.voiceover: One point each. Colombia and Uzbekistan still to come. This group is wide open.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, deep black with gold accents, a clean broadcast standings table, bold white condensed type. Premium, no faces, no logos.

scenes.voiceover: Your daily World Cup story. Every game, every shock — don't miss the next one.
scenes.graphic_prompt: Vertical 9:16, deep black, gold gradient accents, bold white condensed 'DAILY WORLD CUP' lockup with the TikiTaka monogram, night-stadium glow behind. Clean, premium, no faces.

comment_bait: Portugal dropped points to a debutant. Who's actually winning Group K now? Colombia lurking 👀
cta: Subscribe for your daily World Cup story
music_mood: Cinematic, building — a tense floodlit intro that swells into a defiant, historic anthem. Underdog energy.

thumbnail.concept: Deep black and gold blowout, bold white 'POR 1-1 COD' scoreboard, a single gold 'FIRST EVER WC GOAL' stamp, DR Congo flag silhouette on the left against an erupting-crowd glow — statement of history, not two flags.

youtube_title: Yoane Wissa scores DR Congo's FIRST EVER World Cup goal in 1-1 draw vs Portugal | WC 2026
youtube_description: DR Congo ended a 52-year World Cup drought in style. Playing their first World Cup match since 1974 (when they qualified as Zaire), the Congolese held one of the tournament favorites — Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal — to a 1-1 draw in Houston. Joao Neves gave Portugal a 5th-minute lead, but Yoane Wissa equalized with DR Congo's first ever World Cup goal, writing his name into football history. Portugal pushed for a winner with Ronaldo, Bruno Fernandes, and Rafael Leao — but DR Congo's defense held firm. Group K is wide open with Colombia and Uzbekistan still to play. Your daily World Cup stories, every day. #worldcup2026 #portugal #drcongo
instagram_caption: 52 years without a World Cup goal. Then Yoane Wissa changed everything. 🇨🇩 Portugal 1-1 DR Congo — history made in Houston. Portugal dropped points to a debutant — who's actually winning Group K now? Colombia lurking 👇 Subscribe for your daily World Cup story.
